title: Plugin chain P5: orchestrator ship + activation (generated project CLAUDE.md + backstop skill + reconcile orchestrator.md)
description:
Fifth ticket of the plugin packaging chain (epic TASK-020). Per `tasks/TASK-020.research.md` §C and §H. The blocking constraint: a plugin-root `CLAUDE.md` is NOT loaded as context, so the orchestrator (RESUME FIRST + First-chat routing) must be activated another way. Recommended approach (Option 3): `/init-project` generates/merges a project-level `CLAUDE.md`, backed by an always-on routing skill.

**Human decision locked:** the project-CLAUDE.md write uses **PROMPT-ONCE then marker-delimited merge** — ask the user before the first write; if they consent, append a fenced `<!-- BEGIN agentic-framework routing -->` / `<!-- END agentic-framework routing -->` block; re-runs replace ONLY that block; the user's existing content is never clobbered.

Scope:
1. `/init-project` (P4) writes the RESUME-FIRST four-step sequence + First-chat routing + workflow loop + repo etiquette into `${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/CLAUDE.md`: if absent → full block (after prompt); if present → prompt once, then marker-merge.
2. Ship an always-on routing backstop skill (`skills/orchestrator-routing/SKILL.md`) carrying the RESUME-FIRST sequence, for the 'installed but not yet init-ed, fresh chat' case.
3. **Reconcile `agents/orchestrator.md` with the v2 pointer/bundle contract (§H)** — the current file still documents the v1 single-file `state/session.json` + `state/sessions/<updated_at>.json` archive model, which contradicts `CLAUDE.md` + `state/README.md`. Shipping the stale text would propagate the wrong session model into every user project.
acceptance_criteria: init-project writes RESUME-FIRST + First-chat routing into ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/CLAUDE.md. Tests cover four cases: (a) absent → prompt then full block; (b) present + consent → prompt then marker-delimited append; (c) present + decline → no write; (d) re-run → replaces ONLY the marked block, idempotent.
Collision safety: a user CLAUDE.md with pre-existing content is preserved byte-for-byte OUTSIDE the marker block across an init and a re-init. Test asserts this.
An always-on routing backstop skill ships (skills/orchestrator-routing/) carrying the RESUME-FIRST sequence; `claude plugin details` shows it in the inventory.

`agents/orchestrator.md` is reconciled to the v2 pointer/bundle model — a grep confirms zero remaining references to the v1 single-file session.json / <updated_at>.json archive scheme, and the agent text matches the contract in CLAUDE.md + state/README.md.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Minted from TASK-020 spike §F (P5) + §C/§H. Carries the human-locked CLAUDE.md policy (prompt-once-then-marker-merge). AC4 reconciles the stale v1 orchestrator.md — the spike flagged this as the highest-risk correctness item in the whole package; do not ship it unreconciled.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: CLOSED APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS (auto-close per standing plugin-chain authorization — green review, 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM). Tests-first 5d5c403 (4 spec files, 24 failing for the right reasons); impl 715abc4. Reviewer (fresh context): 0 HIGH / 0 MEDIUM / 2 LOW / 1 NIT, full suite 298 passed / 2 skipped DETERMINISTIC across two clean runs, dist/init.cjs == fresh build, exactly one shebang, v1-token grep across BOTH orchestrator.md copies = zero. ALL FOUR ACs MET. Delivered: (1) src/claude-md.js — pure mergeRoutingBlock(existing|null, routing) with exact markers `<!-- BEGIN/END agentic-framework routing -->`: absent→full block, present-no-marker→byte-preserving append, present-with-block→replace-only-the-block idempotent; routingBlockContent() carries v2 RESUME-FIRST + first-chat routing + workflow + etiquette. (2) Consent wired into runInit SEPARATE from wizard intake answers: answers-mode needs explicit signal (--claude-md-consent flag OR claude_md_consent:true) and writes WITHOUT prompting; interactive prompts EXACTLY once and only when no marker block exists (re-run replaces silently). Reviewer scrutinized the consent-swallow: writeOrchestratorRouting is OUTSIDE the try/catch and the production prompter is null (guard returns before catch), so a swallowed throw can never mask a write error — worst case is a no-write, CLAUDE.md never corrupted. (3) Always-on backstop skill skills/orchestrator-routing/SKILL.md (+ .claude/skills/ byte-identical mirror) — now visible in plugin inventory. (4) BOTH orchestrator.md copies reconciled v1→v2 (pointer+bundle model), byte-identical per agents-parity, cross-checked consistent with CLAUDE.md + state/README.md + session.schema.json — no new contradiction. AUTHORIZED existing-spec edit: tests/plugin-scaffold.spec.js skills-inventory assertion updated from [tech-training-template] to [orchestrator-routing, tech-training-template] — legitimate inventory change (skill genuinely ships), reviewer confirmed it strengthens (still exact-list, still excludes global skills), no other spec touched. commands/init-project.md gained a consent-step note. 2 LOWs DEFERRED without a follow-up ticket per chain close pattern (both non-blocking, framework never produces the states): (L1) mergeRoutingBlock degrades safely on adversarial/hand-corrupted markers (BEGIN-without-END appends a 2nd block) — robustness-only, user bytes always preserved, never throws; (L2) the bare catch on the consent prompter treats any throw as decline — tiny blast radius, consider narrowing/logging later. NIT: no explicit absent-file + interactive-consent matrix cell (covered by code-trace + the two adjacent cells). Net new tests +28 (270 → 298). Operational follow-up still pending for the chain: live `claude plugin details` sensor (run at install) — confirms the new skill + /init-project register.
